title: On Jean-Marie Souriau's geometric quantization of the relativistic electron
abstract: The aim of this paper is to revisit, in Souriau's book "Structure of Dynamical Systems", the chapter devoted to the geometric quantization where the justifications of important results and formulae are not given and are difficult to prove. After recalling the coadjoint orbit method and its application to the relativistic particle with spin, we state and prove two keystone theorems that allow to equip its prequantum manifold with the symplectic and contact structures. We apply them to the relativistic particle with spin 1/2, leading to the Dirac equation and the conservation of the probability current. We propose also an identity of conservation of the spin current and, invoking the Kaluza-Klein theory, a systematic construction of the symmetries of charge conjugation, parity transformation and time reversal which seems to us more convenient and readable than that of the classical presentations.
summary: This paper provides a rigorous mathematical re-examination of geometric quantization for the relativistic electron, building on Souriau's work. It successfully derives the Dirac equation and proves the conservation of both probability and spin currents. The work is significant for its systematic use of advanced mathematical tools like coadjoint orbits and Kaluza-Klein theory to establish fundamental symmetries.
main_problem: The paper revisits and rigorously justifies Jean-Marie Souriau's geometric quantization procedure applied to the relativistic electron, addressing ambiguities in the original formulation.
main_result: The authors successfully derive the Dirac equation and establish the conservation laws for both the probability and spin currents using a systematic geometric quantization framework.
method: The core method involves applying the coadjoint orbit method, equipping the prequantum manifold with symplectic and contact structures, and utilizing Kaluza-Klein theory to systematically construct symmetries.
model_or_system: The system is the relativistic particle with spin 1/2 (the electron), analyzed using the Poincaré group and formulated via quaternionic matrices and spinor representations.
key_observables: Dirac equation, conservation of probability current, conservation of spin current, and the systematic construction of $C, P, T$ symmetries.
important_parameters: Rest mass ($m_0$), spin ($	ext{spin}$), and the quantization constant $\hbar$.
main_assumptions: The existence of a prequantum manifold $Y$ and the applicability of the Sommerfeld quantization condition are key assumptions.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the prequantum manifold structure for the electron and positron, and tables summarize the action of symmetry transformations ($C, P, T$) on spinors and coordinates.
paper_structure: The paper progresses by reviewing the coadjoint orbit method, establishing keystone theorems for symplectic/contact structures, applying these to the spin-1/2 case to derive the Dirac equation, and finally using Kaluza-Klein theory for symmetry construction.
why_interesting: While highly abstract, the rigorous development of conserved currents and symmetries ($C, P, T$) using advanced geometric quantization techniques provides a deep theoretical underpinning for relativistic quantum field theory, which informs many modern quantum theories.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
